<!-- Generated by ac:plan-worker-junior on 2026-05-21. Adapted from fluttersdk_artisan copilot-instructions.md for the fluttersdk_telescope release-prep plan. -->

# GitHub Copilot Instructions

This file provides guidance to GitHub Copilot when working with code in this repository. The conventions below also apply to other AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor) operating against telescope.

## Stack

Flutter SDK package. Requires the Flutter runtime (widgets, foundation, dart:ui) unlike the pure-Dart artisan
framework. Dart 3.4+ / Flutter 3.22+. Plugin of `fluttersdk_artisan`: contributes `TelescopeArtisanProvider` which
surfaces 6 CLI commands (`telescope:install`, `telescope:tail`, `telescope:requests`, `telescope:queries`,
`telescope:caches`, `telescope:clear`) and 9 MCP tools backed by `ext.telescope.*` VM Service extensions.

Deps: `fluttersdk_artisan` (path), `logging: ^1.2.0`, `meta: ^1.16.0`. Dev deps: `flutter_test`, `flutter_lints`,
`magic` (path, dev-only for watcher integration tests). This package is debug-only at the consumer call site: the
consumer wraps `TelescopePlugin.install()` inside `if (kDebugMode)` so release builds tree-shake the entire subsystem.

The package ships its own Flutter-free CLI entry point (`bin/fluttersdk_telescope.dart`) so
`dart run fluttersdk_telescope:mcp`-style invocations work without dragging `dart:ui` into a pure-Dart context,
and a `lib/cli.dart` codegen barrel (typedef `FluttersdkTelescopeArtisanProvider`) consumed by consumer-side
`lib/app/_plugins.g.dart` auto-discovery. An `install.yaml` manifest at the package root makes
`plugin:install fluttersdk_telescope` work end-to-end via the artisan PluginInstaller.

## Commands

| Command | When |
|---|---|
| `flutter test` | Run all tests (baseline 307+ green after alpha-3). |
| `flutter test --coverage` | Generate coverage data; verify line coverage stays at or above 80%. |
| `dart format lib/ test/` | Format. Must produce no diff. |
| `dart analyze` | Static analysis. Zero issues required across `lib/` and `test/`. |
| `dart pub get` | Resolve deps after pubspec.yaml changes. |
| `dart run fluttersdk_telescope <cmd>` | Run a `telescope:*` command standalone (Flutter-free CLI wrapper at `bin/fluttersdk_telescope.dart`). |

## Golden Rules (apply on every change)

1. **Test coverage stays at or above 80%**. Run `flutter test --coverage` after behavioral changes and verify the
   line coverage ratio does not drop below 80%. Drops below this floor block the change.
2. **CHANGELOG always under `[Unreleased]`**. Every behavioral or interface change lands an entry under
   `## [Unreleased]` in `CHANGELOG.md`. Categories: `Added` / `Changed` / `Fixed` / `Removed`. Promote to a dated
   section on `dart pub publish`.
3. **Green gate plus TDD**. `dart format lib/ test/` produces zero diff, `dart analyze` returns zero issues,
   `flutter test` returns all green. TDD red-green-refactor for behavioral changes: write the failing test first,
   then the implementation that turns it green. Reverting the implementation must turn the test red again.
4. **README sync**. When a change is significant enough for the package landing page (new watcher type, new MCP
   tool surface, new install flow, breaking change), update `README.md`. Use descriptive link labels.

## Architecture

Single barrel: `lib/telescope.dart` re-exports the full public API. Subsystem layout under `lib/src/`:

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `watchers/` | `TelescopeWatcher` abstract contract + concrete impls (`LogWatcher`, `ExceptionWatcher`, `DumpWatcher`). |
| `adapters/` | `TelescopeHttpAdapter` abstract contract + `DioHttpAdapter` concrete impl. |
| `records/` | Immutable record types: `HttpRequestRecord`, `LogRecordEntry`, `ExceptionRecord`, `MagicModelRecord`, `MagicCacheRecord`, `EventRecord`, `GateRecord`, `DumpRecord`, `QueryRecord`. |
| `extensions/` | `registerAllTelescopeExtensions()` aggregator + per-concern VM Service handlers (incl. `queriesHandler`, `cachesHandler`). |
| `commands/` | `TelescopeInstallCommand`, `TelescopeTailCommand`, `TelescopeRequestsCommand`, `TelescopeQueriesCommand`, `TelescopeCachesCommand`, `TelescopeClearCommand`. |
| `telescope_store.dart` | 9-buffer ring store (singleton). `Queue<T>` per buffer + broadcast `StreamController<T>` per buffer. |
| `telescope_plugin.dart` | `TelescopePlugin.install()` entry + `registerHttpAdapter()` + `registerWatcher()` extension points. |
| `telescope_artisan_provider.dart` | `TelescopeArtisanProvider extends ArtisanServiceProvider`: 6 commands + 9 MCP tool descriptors. |
| `bin/fluttersdk_telescope.dart` | Flutter-free CLI wrapper (no `dart:ui` import); runs the provider's commands standalone via `dart run fluttersdk_telescope`. |
| `lib/cli.dart` | Flutter-free codegen barrel with `FluttersdkTelescopeArtisanProvider` typedef alias; consumed by consumer-side `lib/app/_plugins.g.dart` auto-discovery. |
| `install.yaml` | V1 plugin manifest (empty publish list + post-install bootstrap message) required by `plugin:install fluttersdk_telescope`. |

### Three public contracts

- `TelescopeWatcher`: `name` getter, `install()`, `uninstall()`. Magic ships `MagicModelWatcher`,
  `MagicCacheWatcher`, `MagicEventWatcher`, `MagicGateWatcher`, `MagicQueryWatcher` via this contract.
- `TelescopeHttpAdapter`: same 3-method shape (`name`, `install`, `uninstall`). Magic ships
  `MagicHttpFacadeAdapter` via this contract.
- `TelescopePlugin`: `install()`, `registerHttpAdapter()`, `registerWatcher()` are the consumer-facing entry points.

All three contracts are frozen. Magic-side glue depends on them directly; any signature change requires a
coordinated bump across both repos.

### VM Service surface

`ext.telescope.requests`, `ext.telescope.console`, `ext.telescope.exceptions`, `ext.telescope.events`,
`ext.telescope.gates`, `ext.telescope.dumps`, `ext.telescope.queries`, `ext.telescope.caches`,
`ext.telescope.clear`, `ext.telescope.pause`, `ext.telescope.resume`. Every registration goes through
`registerExtensionIdempotent` (from `fluttersdk_artisan`) for hot-restart safety.

### Example apps

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `example/` | Vanilla path: Dio adapter + `package:logging` sink + `ExceptionWatcher` + `DumpWatcher`. |
| `example_magic/` | Magic-managed path: `MagicHttpFacadeAdapter` + `MagicModelWatcher` + `MagicEventWatcher` + `MagicGateWatcher`. |

## Conventions

- `final class` on new concrete record types and watcher impls. `abstract class` for the two contracts
  (`TelescopeWatcher`, `TelescopeHttpAdapter`).
- `Queue<T>` (not `List<T>`) for ring buffers. O(1) add/remove at both ends.
- Chain-preserve every global handler override: save the previous handler, call it inside the new handler, restore
  on uninstall. This is how Sentry and Bugsnag coexist with telescope's `ExceptionWatcher` and `DumpWatcher`.
- Every `developer.registerExtension` call goes through `registerExtensionIdempotent`. Duplicate registration on
  hot-restart would throw `ArgumentError` without this guard.
- `kDebugMode` gate at consumer install sites. Watcher `install()` bodies themselves may also guard, but the
  primary gate belongs to the consumer's `main.dart`.
- VM Service handler signature: `Future<ServiceExtensionResponse> Function(String method, Map<String, String> params)`.
  Parse integers via `int.tryParse(params['key'] ?? '')`. Return `.result(jsonEncode(payload))` or
  `.error(kInvalidParams, msg)`.
- **MCP tool descriptions** follow Claude Code canonical format: imperative opening sentence, brief context
  paragraph, `Usage:` bullet list. Critical info first (CC truncates MCP descriptions at 2 KB chars).
- **TDD strict**: every behavioral change arrives as a failing test first. Red then green, no exceptions.
- **No mockito**. Stub via contract inheritance or plain fakes inside the test file. `flutter_test` is the only
  test framework.
- `TelescopeStore.resetForTesting()` in `tearDown` to prevent buffer state leaking between tests.
- Numbered step comments (`// 1.`, `// 2.`) for watcher `install()` bodies that chain-preserve handlers (they all
  have 2-3 distinct phases: save previous, replace, guard). Docblocks (`///`) on public classes and the abstract
  contracts only; skip decorative docstrings on trivial getters.
- **Forbidden keywords in artifacts**: no em-dash (U+2014) or en-dash (U+2013); use comma, colon, semicolon,
  period, or parentheses instead.

## Off-limits

- `TelescopeHttpAdapter` and `TelescopeWatcher` abstract contract signatures (`name`, `install`, `uninstall`) are
  frozen. Magic-side glue depends on them. Any change requires a coordinated bump across both repos.
- `TelescopeHttpAdapter.pendingCount` is an ADDITIVE optional method with a default body returning 0; the three
  frozen signatures above stay frozen. Implementations using `extends TelescopeHttpAdapter` inherit the default;
  implementations using `implements TelescopeHttpAdapter` must add an explicit `int get pendingCount => 0;` override.
- `TelescopePlugin.install()`, `.registerHttpAdapter()`, `.registerWatcher()` signatures are frozen for the same
  reason.
- `TelescopeStore` public method signatures (`recordHttp`, `recordLog`, `recordException`, `recordMagicModel`,
  `recordMagicCache`, `recordEvent`, `recordGate`, `recordDump`, `recordQuery` and their `recent*` / `on*Record`
  counterparts) are frozen; magic-side calls them directly.
- No CLI command additions for events, gates, or dumps. MCP-only access for these three watchers is intentional;
  CLI parity remains V1.x backlog. DB queries and caches do have CLI commands as of alpha-3.
- `install.yaml` manifest at the package root is required for `plugin:install fluttersdk_telescope`. Do not delete
  it; the V1 manifest carries the post-install bootstrap message and the `executables:` mapping anchor.
- No new production dependencies beyond `fluttersdk_artisan`, `logging`, and `meta`. Example apps may add their
  own demo deps (Dio for vanilla, `magic` for `example_magic`).
- `DumpWatcher` must not capture in release builds. The `kDebugMode` guard at install time is load-bearing.
- `example/` and `example_magic/` are dev playgrounds for live e2e validation. Production code does not depend
  on them.
